anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction combines primordial, spontaneously present awareness with repeated instructions to meditate, observe karmic law, keep vows, cultivate devotion, and apply practice until self-grasping fades.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence and blocks candidate-discovery status. The model remains useful as a coding heuristic, but the stance categories need multi-value coding, stage coding, and an analyst-frame-refused option.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: This is not neatly regulatory, dissolutive, or ontological. It may be simultaneous rather than mixed-by-stage. If one primary text requires multiple stance codes at the same time, the model's categories may be heuristic tags rather than stable structural types.

claim_units: Support locus can be coded by function: receiving, agency, memory, reflexivity, integration, teacher, vow, text, community, present act, awareness itself, or later integration.
The forbidden-claimant column is valid only where a tradition uses a regulatory grammar of identifiable agents, permissions, and prohibitions.
Dissolutive traditions do not merely prohibit appropriation, they unsettle the agent-action or subject-object frame that makes appropriation intelligible.
Ontological traditions deny that the decisive reality is produced, acquired, or missing, so practice removes obstruction rather than creating the goal.
Mixed-by-stage traditions shift claimability logic across phases, for example sudden awakening followed by gradual cultivation.
Stance should predict distinct failure modes: wrong claimant, formalism, reification, premature realization, passivity, or stage-transition confusion.

cross_domain_prediction.domain: Psychotherapy and coaching models of client agency
cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Therapies with similar techniques but different agency-frame stance should produce different failure modes. Agency-strengthening models should risk self-blame, surrender-based models should risk passivity, self-dissolving models should risk dissociation or avoidance, and already-whole models should risk bypassing concrete behavioral repair.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Support locus becomes the location of change functions: client, therapist, method, group, medication, environment, insight, or later integration. Claimability stance becomes the model's prior relation to agency: strengthen agency, share agency, dissolve a self-story, treat health as already present but obstructed, or shift stance by stage.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: Failure patterns are explained by alliance, diagnosis, therapist skill, treatment dose, socioeconomic pressure, or trauma history with no additional predictive value from agency-frame stance.

exact_claim: A comparative practice rubric should first ask how a tradition relates to claim-making itself. Support locus and forbidden claimant are useful only after coding the tradition's claimability stance as regulatory, dissolutive, ontological, or mixed-by-stage. That prior stance should predict the tradition's characteristic safeguards and failure modes.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: If coders classify a text as dissolutive from doctrinal passages alone, later manual warnings should target attainment-reification, subject-object fixation, or formalism more than wrong effort level or wrong credit assignment.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Claimability stance predicts warning and repair patterns beyond credit distribution and support locus.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source blind coding of doctrinal passages, then separate warning sections and teacher interviews.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Warnings are generic across traditions or are better predicted by teacher style, institution, intensity of practice, trauma risk, or ordinary upaya pedagogy.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Srivaishnava sub-schools should share a broadly regulatory claimability stance while differing on how effort and grace are distributed.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Claimability stance and credit distribution are separable variables.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Manual coding of Vadakalai and Tenkalai prapatti sources, with coders blinded to the proposed model.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The stance changes whenever credit distribution changes, making the new variable redundant.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Advaita and Dzogchen teachers should report recurring cases where students use already-present language to avoid preparation, conduct, integration, or verification.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Ontological denial produces a distinctive premature-realization failure mode.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Practitioner interviews, adverse-practice reports, and comparison with spiritual-bypassing literature.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: These cases are no more common than in regulatory traditions, or spiritual bypassing explains them without reference to ontological claimability.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: In Chinul-like or Zen training contexts, confusion should cluster around the movement from recognition or sudden awakening into gradual cultivation and integration.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Longitudinal practitioner reports and teacher case interviews coded against baseline risk factors.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Confusion clusters around retreat intensity, social isolation, teacher misconduct, or mental health factors rather than stance transitions.

literature_search_queries: Pargament religion problem-solving process three styles self-directing deferring collaborative
George Lindbeck doctrine as grammar rules cultural linguistic model religion
Dogen Bendowa practice realization one practice-realization
Longchen Rabjam Trekcho primordial state rigpa nonfabrication
Advaita praptasya prapti already attained realization practice removes ignorance
Srivaishnava markata marjara nyaya prapatti grace effort
Chinul Secrets on Cultivating the Mind sudden awakening gradual cultivation
Buddhist upaya skillful means capacities pedagogy
spiritual bypassing nondual premature realization claims

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate narrows grammar to practice appropriation and asks whether the grammar authorizes, dissolves, or denies claim-making.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction. The grammar-level move is known; the candidate's remaining contribution is an operational sub-rubric for practice functions.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the idea that doctrine functions as communally authoritative rules for discourse, attitude, and action rather than only propositions or symbols.
near_neighbors.source: George Lindbeck, The Nature of Doctrine, rule or grammar theory summarized in https://repository.up.ac.za/bitstream/handle/2263/64227/Kim_Study_2018.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds traditions that refuse the agency frame itself and separates claimability stance from credit distribution.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate to large reduction for regulatory traditions. Less reduction for dissolutive and ontological cases.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for distributing agency between human actor and God through self-directing, deferring, and collaborative styles.
near_neighbors.source: Kenneth Pargament et al., Religion and the Problem-Solving Process: Three Styles of Coping, https://icmglt.org/icmlibrary/religion-and-the-problem-solving-process-three-styles-of-coping/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate generalizes this as one possible stance in a cross-tradition rubric.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The insight is old; the comparative use may be new.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close primary-text source for the dissolutive category, since agent and action are not independently grounded.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate codes this as dissolutive claimability and predicts formalism or attainment-reification failure modes.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The Dogen structure is known; the failure-mode prediction needs testing.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for practice-realization identity and resistance to treating realization as a later object possessed by a practitioner.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate treats Dzogchen as ontological denial of produced acquisition, but Longchenpa also preserves regulatory practice pressures.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction and strong anomaly pressure, because one text can mix stance values without a clean stage shift.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for primordial, self-arisen awareness and nonfabrication, while still giving concrete instructions, vows, devotion, and conduct safeguards.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate turns this into a cross-tradition ontological claimability value.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. The ontological category is inherited, not discovered.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for attainment of what is already attained and practice as removal of ignorance rather than production of reality.
near_neighbors.source: Advaita Vedanta praptasya prapti discussions, example source https://realization.org/down/mountain-path/53-2.2016-April.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate can use the debate to test separability between regulatory claimability and credit distribution.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction but good test value. The variable is not merely an academic invention.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for indigenous analysis of grace, effort, surrender, and the burden of agency.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate formalizes stage shifts as mixed-by-stage claimability.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Large reduction for the mixed category unless the model defines what it cannot absorb.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for sudden awakening followed by gradual cultivation, a primary case of stage-dependent claimability.
near_neighbors.source: Chinul, Secrets on Cultivating the Mind, Buswell translation, https://www.themathesontrust.org/papers/buddhism/Chinul-Secrets_on_Cultivating_the_Mind.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate focuses on appropriation, support locus, and failure repair rather than general pedagogical adaptation.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate reduction. Some predictive burden may already belong to upaya pedagogy.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close for adapting practices and teachings to capacity, context, and stage.
near_neighbors.source: Buddhist upaya and skillful means pedagogy, example search result https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s12671-021-01795-2

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds claimability stance as a prior validity condition and limits the forbidden-claimant column to regulatory grammars.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: High internal reduction. Treat as consolidation and extension, not as a standalone discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close for support locus, forbidden claimant, credit distribution, agency handoff, safeguards, and failure modes.
near_neighbors.source: Prior Lumenary records supplied in prompt: Forbidden Claimant Rubric, Credit Distribution, Capacity Ledger, Handoff Point, Entry-Claimant Grammar

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat it as a comparative and practitioner-facing coding heuristic.
Build a coding sheet with fields for function, support locus, forbidden claimant, claimability stance, stage, safeguard, failure mode, evidence passage, analyst-frame refusal, and confidence.

Allow multiple simultaneous stance values. Add regulatory, dissolutive, ontological, mixed-by-stage, simultaneous-mixed, practice-realization, non-acquisition, no-claiming-frame, analyst-frame-refused, and undecidable.
Define limiting cases for mixed-by-stage and simultaneous-mixed before further use. Name what the model cannot code.
Run a split-source blind test: code stance from doctrinal or entry passages, then predict warnings and repairs from separate manuals or interviews.

Compare predictive power against Lindbeck grammar, Pargament agency styles, upaya pedagogy, grace-effort theology, spiritual bypassing literature, prior Capacity Ledger, Forbidden Claimant, Credit Distribution, Handoff Point, and Entry-Claimant Grammar.
Use Longchenpa Trekcho, Dogen practice-realization, inherited religion, Pure Land Other Power, Srivaishnava prapatti, and successful cross-training as anomaly tests rather than confirming examples.
Ask practitioners for concrete failed-student cases and repair instructions, not agreement with the model.
Do not raise above novel_synthesis unless blind coding shows that claimability stance predicts safeguards, repairs, or failure modes better than existing models and prior Lumenary ledgers.
novelty_adjustment: Reduce novelty from 0.68 to about 0.60. The idea is not a discovery of anti-appropriation, non-doership, practice-realization, already-attained realization, grace-effort distribution, or stage-dependent cultivation. Those are well anticipated by primary texts, Lindbeck, Pargament, upaya pedagogy, and prior Lumenary records. The remaining contribution is a novel synthesis: claimability stance as a prior validity check for support-locus and forbidden-claimant coding.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Practitioners say this is ordinary doctrine-as-grammar, religious coping, upaya, grace-effort theology, nondual pedagogy, spiritual bypassing, or prior Lumenary ledger language. Dissolutive and ontological teachers reject the rubric as reifying. Teachers cannot produce stance-specific repairs beyond familiar cautions against pride, laziness, premature claims, or poor integration.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners independently accept the stance distinction, code it without major distortion, and use it to predict concrete safeguards, repair instructions, screening decisions, or transition risks that support locus and credit distribution alone miss.
questions: Is claimability stance a real distinction in your teaching work, or does it impose an outsider frame?
When students fail, do repairs change who claims practice, dissolve the claiming structure, correct premature non-acquisition language, or manage a stage transition?
Can you fill support-locus cells without distorting your tradition's own language?
Does this rubric predict a concrete warning or repair before consulting a manual, or does it rename familiar upaya, grace, agency, or lineage pedagogy?
Can you describe one student failure case that this rubric explains better than ordinary effort, faith, morality, trauma, teacher fit, or community support?
relevant_practitioners: Soto Zen teachers familiar with Dogen's practice-realization
Madhyamaka scholars or Buddhist teachers familiar with agent-action analysis
Dzogchen or Mahamudra teachers familiar with direct introduction
Advaita Vedanta teachers familiar with praptasya prapti
Jodo Shinshu ministers and Shinran scholars
Theravada meditation teachers familiar with right effort and anatta
Srivaishnava scholars or practitioners familiar with prapatti debates
Sufi teachers familiar with maqam and hal
Contemplative clinicians familiar with spiritual bypassing and adverse meditation effects

primary_pair: Nagarjuna, Mulamadhyamakakarika chapter 8, Examination of Agent and Action, Padmakara translation, https://www.gileht.site/mulamadhyamakakarika/kakarika-verses-ptg
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: High. The rubric improves the earlier forbidden-claimant model, but claimability stance may still be an analyst's frame. The mixed-by-stage category could absorb counterexamples unless limiting cases are defined. The model also risks coding support locus in traditions where separating support, actor, action, and realization is itself part of the error being criticized.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Search reached Pargament on religious problem-solving styles, Lindbeck on doctrine as rule or grammar, Dogen practice-realization sources, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, Chinul sudden awakening and gradual cultivation, Srivaishnava prapatti debates, Gita action-doership passages, Shinran Other Power, and upaya literature. It did not fully close-read Lindbeck's full book, Pargament's full 1988 article, Tibetan commentarial material on direct introduction, Sanskrit Advaita sources on praptasya prapti, or Srivaishnava primary polemics. This blocks candidate-discovery status.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: MMK 8 attacks any independently established agent-action structure. Bendowa collapses a simple before-after relation between practice and realization, while still insisting on concrete sitting, teacher transmission, and trust. Together they support the candidate's warning that prohibition language can distort dissolutive traditions. They also reduce novelty, because the anti-appropriation move is primary-textual rather than newly discovered. The possible contribution is the comparative coding rule: do not use forbidden claimant until the tradition's claimability grammar has been identified.

title: The Claimability and Support Rubric
unlike_statement: Unlike Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar, Pargament's religious agency styles, Nagarjuna's agent-action critique, Dogen's practice-realization, Dzogchen primordial purity, Advaita praptasya prapti, Srivaishnava effort-grace debates, upaya pedagogy, and prior Lumenary credit-distribution records, this idea says the first coding question is whether the claiming framework itself is authorized, dissolved, ontologically denied, or stage-dependent. Its possible contribution is not the anti-appropriation insight, but the rule that support-locus and forbidden-claimant rubrics are valid only after that prior stance is coded.
